changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for consistency with naming conventions
review_notes: The functions generally map well to the control of identifying unauthorized use of organizational systems. Audit logging, RBAC, and NetworkPolicies are appropriate mechanisms in Kubernetes to address this control. However, the naming of the audit logging function needed refinement to align with the resource naming conventions.
naming_improvements: Use 'apiserver' as the resource name for audit logging functions to maintain consistency with existing patterns
title: 3_14_7: 3.14.7 Identify unauthorized use of organizational systems
step1_reasoning: The control 3_14_7 focuses on identifying unauthorized use of organizational systems, which can be mapped to Kubernetes through audit logging to track access and actions, RBAC to enforce least privilege, and NetworkPolicies to control ingress traffic. Audit logging in Kubernetes can capture detailed information about API requests, which helps in identifying unauthorized access. RBAC ensures that users have only the permissions they need, reducing the risk of unauthorized actions. NetworkPolicies can restrict unauthorized network access to the cluster.
